steps.title: Extract the equation from the graph
steps.content: Reading two clear points from the line: at $x = -3$, $y = 8$, and at $x = 1$, $y = 4$. The slope is $$\frac{4 - 8}{1 - (-3)} = \frac{-4}{4} = -1$$ Using point-slope form with $(1, 4)$: $$y - 4 = -1(x - 1) \implies y = -x + 5$$

steps.title: Solve for f(x) using the given relationship
steps.content: The graph displays $y = f(x) + 13$, which we found equals $-x + 5$. Therefore: $$f(x) + 13 = -x + 5$$ $$f(x) = -x - 8$$

steps.title: Match to the required form with positive c and d
steps.content:
Rewrite $f(x) = -x - 8$ as $$f(x) = -8 - 1 \cdot x$$ Comparing to the choice forms:
  • Choice A: $f(x) = -d - cx$ becomes $-8 - 1 \cdot x$ when $d = 8$ and $c = 1$ — both positive.
  • Choice B: $f(x) = d - cx$ would require $d = -8$ (negative).
  • Choice C: $f(x) = d + cx$ would require $d = -8$ and $c = -1$ (both negative).
  • Choice D: $f(x) = -d + cx$ would require $c = -1$ (negative).
That matches choice A — A is correct.
